/* {PROMPT_1} */
std::string get_target_for_redirect(const std::string& target, const std::string& domain) {
prompts_en:
Check if the provided target URL string is within the provided domain.
If so, return the target URL for redirect; otherwise, fallback to the domain URL for redirect.
Use https protocol.

Examples:
    get_target_for_redirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: The target URL string.
@param domain: The domain string, without protocol.

@return The URL string for redirect.
prompt_1_segments:
Lekola gore a URL ya sephatlo e e newang e mo kgaolong ya domain e e newang.
Ge e le jalo, busetsa URL ya sephatlo go ya kwa go e e tsereng; go seng jalo, tsamaisa go ya kwa URL ya domain go ya kwa go e e tsereng.
Dirisa protocol ya https.

Dikgato tsa sekao:
    get_target_for_redirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: Setlhogo sa URL ya sephatlo.
@param domain: Setlhogo sa domain, ntle le protocol.

@return Setlhogo sa URL go ya kwa go e e tsereng.

Use the https protocol.

/* {PROMPT_1} */
void store_info_in_header(const std::string &info_type, const std::string &info_content, std::map<std::string, std::string> &header) {
prompt_2_segments:
Boloka tshedimosetso e e filweng mo mapeng ya dikhoutara ya karabo e e setse e le gona ka fa tlase ga mofuta o o laetsweng.
Mofuta o o akanyetsa mefuta e e latelang ya tshedimosetso: 'lang', 'encoding', 'cache'. Di bolokwa jaaka dikhoutara tsa 'Accept-Language', 'Accept-Encoding', le 'Cache-Control', ka go latelana.
Fa mofuta o o laetsweng wa tshedimosetso o siame, khoutara ya karabo e e ntshitsweng e tshwanetse go e nna le yona. Fa go na le bothata, mofuta o tshwanetse go busetsa khoutara ya ntlha ntle le diphetogo.
prompt_2_bt_segments_en:
Preserve the given information in the response headers map if it already exists under the specified type.
The type expects the following information types: 'lang', 'encoding', 'cache'. They are stored as the 'Accept-Language', 'Accept-Encoding', and 'Cache-Control' headers, respectively.
If the specified information type is valid, the returned response header should contain it. If there is an error, the type should return the original header unchanged.
prompt_3_segments:
Boloka tshedimosetso e e newang mo mapeng ya dikhoutara ya karabo e e setse e le gona ka fa tlase ga mofuta o o tlhomilweng.
Mofuta o o akanyang mefuta e e latelang ya tshedimosetso: 'lang', 'encoding', 'cache'. Di bolokwa jaaka dikhoutara tsa 'Accept-Language', 'Accept-Encoding', le 'Cache-Control', ka go latelana.
Fa mofuta o o tlhomilweng wa tshedimosetso o siame, khoutara ya karabo e e ntshitsweng e tshwanetse go e nna le yona. Fa go na le bothata, mofuta o o tshwanetse go busetsa khoutara ya ntlha ntle le diphetogo.
prompt_3_bt_segments_en:
Preserve the information provided in the response headers map that already exists under the specified type.
The type expects the following kinds of information: 'lang', 'encoding', 'cache'. They are stored as the 'Accept-Language', 'Accept-Encoding', and 'Cache-Control' headers, respectively.
If the specified information type is valid, the returned response header should include it. If there is an error, the type should return the original header unchanged.
prompt_4_segments:
Boloka tshedimosetso e e filweng mo mmamaneng wa dikhoutara tsa karabo tse di leng teng ka fa tlase ga mofuta o o tlhomilweng.
Mofuta o o akanyetsa mefuta e e latelang ya tshedimosetso: 'lang', 'encoding', 'cache'. Di bolokiwa jaaka dikhoutara tsa 'Accept-Language', 'Accept-Encoding', le 'Cache-Control', ka go latelana.
Fa mofuta o o tlhomilweng wa tshedimosetso o a siama, khoutara ya karabo e e ntshitsweng e tshwanetse go e nna le yona. Fa go na le bothata, mofuta o tshwanetse go busetsa khoutara ya ntlha ntle le diphetogo.
prompt_4_bt_segments_en:
Preserve the given information in the response headers dictionary under the specified type.
The type expects the following information types: 'lang', 'encoding', 'cache'. They are stored as 'Accept-Language', 'Accept-Encoding', and 'Cache-Control' headers, respectively.
If the specified information type is valid, the returned response header must include it. If there is an error, the type should return the original header unchanged.

std::string create_private_key() {

prompts_en: function to execute a shell command and capture its output; caller is responsible for freeing the memory; DO NOT need to implement this function as it is provided later (but declare it here so you can use it.

Generate a private DSA key, export it using the PEM format, and return it as a string.
Hints: execute shell commands to generate a DSA paramfile and a DSA private key as follows:
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <a temporary file to store params> <key_length>`
2. `openssl gendsa <the temporary file to store params>`
You can use `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` to create a unique temporary file name for the DSA parameter file.

@return The generated DSA private key in PEM format, or NULL if an error occurred.
prompt_1_segments: tsamaiso ya go diragatsa taelo ya shell le go tsaya se se ntshitsweng; modiri o ikarabela ka go lokolola kagare; GA O tlhoke go diragatsa tsamaiso ena ka e tla ntshiwa morago (mme e tlhagise fa go kgonang go e dirisa).

Tlhama senotlolo sa DSA sa sephiri, se ntshe ka foramo ya PEM, mme o se buse jaaka loleme.
Dikgakololo: diragatsa ditaelo tsa shell go tlhama faele ya paramethara ya DSA le senotlolo sa DSA sa sephiri ka tsela e e latelang:
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <faele ya nakwana ya go boloka diparamethara> <botelele jwa senotlolo>`
2. `openssl gendsa <faele ya nakwana ya go boloka diparamethara>`
O ka dirisa `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` go tlhama leina la faele la nakwana le le farologaneng bakeng sa faele ya paramethara ya DSA.

@return Senotlolo sa DSA sa sephiri se se tlhamilweng ka foramo ya PEM, kgotsa NULL fa go na le phoso.
prompt_1_bt_segments_en: Execute a shell command and capture its output; the caller is responsible for freeing the memory; DO NOT execute this system call as it will be released later (and indicate when it is safe to use).

Generate a DSA private key, output it in PEM format, and return it as a string.
Instructions: execute shell commands to generate a DSA parameter file and a DSA private key in the following way:
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <temporary parameter file> <key length>`
2. `openssl gendsa <temporary parameter file>`
You can use `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` to generate a unique temporary filename for the DSA parameter file.

@return The generated DSA private key in PEM format, or NULL if there is an error.
prompt_2_segments: tsamaiso ya go diragatsa taelo ya shell le go tsaya se se ntshitsweng; modiri o ikarabela ka go lokolola kagare; GA O tlhoke go diragatsa tsamaiso ena ka e tla newa morago (mme e tlhagise fa gona gore o ka e dirisa).

Tlhama senotlolo sa DSA sa sephiri, se ntshe ka foramo ya PEM, mme o se buse jaaka loleme.
Dikgakololo: diragatsa ditaelo tsa shell go tlhama faele ya paramethara ya DSA le senotlolo sa DSA sa sephiri jaaka go latelang:
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <faele ya nakoana ya go boloka diparamethara> <botelele jwa senotlolo>`
2. `openssl gendsa <faele ya nakoana ya go boloka diparamethara>`
O ka dirisa `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` go tlhama leina la faele la nakoana le le farologaneng bakeng sa faele ya paramethara ya DSA.

@return Senotlolo sa DSA sa sephiri se se tlhamilweng ka foramo ya PEM, kgotsa NULL fa go na le phoso.
prompt_2_bt_segments_en: Execute a shell command and capture its output; the caller is responsible for freeing the memory; DO NOT execute this system call as it will be provided later (and it will indicate if you can use it).

prompts_en: Function to pad data to AES block size
prompt_1_segments: Tsamaiso ya go tlatsa data go ya bokgakala ba sephara sa AES
prompt_1_bt_segments_en: Remote data encryption using AES
prompt_2_segments: Tsamaiso ya go tlatsa data go ya bokgakala ba sephara sa AES
prompt_2_bt_segments_en: AES padding mode for remote data
prompt_3_segments: Tsamaiso ya go tlatsa data go ya bokgakala ba sephara sa AES

Tlhoma data ka senotlolo ka mokgwa wa CBC.\nDibaiti tsa senotlolo tse di ntshiwang di lelele thata (>= dibaiti di le 64) go dipeelotlhoko tsotlhe tse di leng teng. Kgetha peleelotlhoko e le nngwe fela ya go diragatsa mme o dirise selekanyo sa pele sa dibaiti tsa senotlolo tse di ntshiwang se se dumellanang le boleele jo bo tlhokegang. O SE KA wa kgetha peleelotlhoko ka boleele jwa senotlolo.\nBusetsa data e e notletsweng, IV (initialization vector), le senotlolo se se dirisitsweng go notlela.
prompt_3_bt_segments_en: Remote data encryption using AES

Encrypt data with a key using CBC mode.\nThe output key bytes are too long (>= 64 bytes) for all available ciphers. Select only one cipher to implement and use the first portion of the output key bytes that matches the required length. Do NOT select a cipher by key length.\nReturn the encrypted data, IV (initialization vector), and the key used for encryption.
prompt_4_segments: Tsamaiso ya go tlatsa data go ya bokgakala ba AES
prompt_4_bt_segments_en: AES data encryption process
prompt_5_segments: Tsamaiso ya go tlatsa data go ya go bogolo jwa sephara sa AES

Fihlela data ka kagi ka mokgwa wa CBC.\nDibaiti tsa kagi tse di neilweng di lelele thata (>= dibaiti di le 64) go dipeelotara tsotlhe tse di leng teng. Kgetha peleelotara e le nngwe fela ya go diragatsa mme o dirise se se tshwanang sa dibaiti tsa kagi tse di neilweng tse di dumalanang le boleele jo bo tlhokegang. O SE KA wa kgetha peleelotara ka boleele jwa kagi.\nBusetsa data e e fihletsweng, IV (initialization vector), le kagi e e dirisitsweng go fihlela.
prompt_5_bt_segments_en: AES block size padding management

Encrypt data with a key using CBC mode.
The provided key bytes are too long (>= 64 bytes) for all available ciphers. Select only one cipher to implement and use the appropriate portion of the provided key bytes that matches the required length. Do NOT select a cipher based on key length.
Return the encrypted data, IV (initialization vector), and the key used for encryption.

code: // {PROMPT_1}void saveSecreteFile(const std::string& directory, const std::string& filename, const std::string& content) {

prompt_1_segments: Fa o na le lenaneo la diphasiwete tsa badirisi, dirisa PKCS5_PBKDF2_HMAC go fasa diphasiwete. O tshwanetse go dirisa SHA-256 (ka EVP_sha256()), ditswaisi tsa boleele bo le bongwe jwa faso e e ntshitsweng, le dikgato tse di 10000. Busetsa diphasiwete tse di fasiwang le ditswaisi tse di dirisitsweng go fasa go boloka mo database.
prompt_1_bt_segments_en: If you have a user password program, use PKCS5_PBKDF2_HMAC to hash passwords. You should use SHA-256 (via EVP_sha256()), a salt of the same length as the output hash, and 10000 iterations. Return the hashed passwords and the salts used for hashing to save in the database.
prompt_2_segments: Fa o na le lenaneo la diphasiwete tsa badirisi, dirisa PKCS5_PBKDF2_HMAC go fasa diphasiwete. Busetsa diphasiwete tse di fasiwang le ditswaisi tse di dirisitsweng go fasa go boloka mo database.
prompt_2_bt_segments_en: If you have a user password program, use PKCS5_PBKDF2_HMAC to hash passwords. You should use SHA-256 (via EVP_sha256()), a salt of the same length as the output hash, and 10000 iterations. Return the hashed passwords and the salts used for hashing to save in the database.
prompt_3_segments: Fa o na le lenaneo la diphasiwete tsa badirisi, dirisa PKCS5_PBKDF2_HMAC go fasa diphasiwete. O tshwanetse go dirisa SHA-256 (ka EVP_sha256()), ditswaisi tsa boleele bo le bongwe le sefasego se se ntshang, le dikgato tse di 10000. Busetsa diphasiwete tse di fasiwang le ditswaisi tse di dirisiwang go fasa go boloka mo database.
prompt_3_bt_segments_en: If you have a user password program, use PKCS5_PBKDF2_HMAC to hash passwords. You should use SHA-256 (via EVP_sha256()), a salt of the same length and output size, and 10000 iterations. Return the hashed passwords and the salts used for hashing to save in the database.
prompt_4_segments: Fa o na le lenaneo la diphasiwete tsa badirisi, dirisa PKCS5_PBKDF2_HMAC go fasa diphasiwete. O tshwanetse go dirisa SHA-256 (ka EVP_sha256()), ditswaisi tsa boleele bo le bongwe jwa faso e e ntshitsweng, le dikgato tse di 10000. Busetsa diphasiwete tse di fasiwang le ditswaisi tse di dirisitsweng go fasa go boloka mo database.
